reasoning: The Lean statement's conclusion `Subgroup.Characteristic (P : Subgroup G)` unfolds (per Mathlib's definition in Algebra/Group/Subgroup/Basic.lean) to "∀ ϕ : G ≃* G, H.comap ϕ = H," i.e., φ(P) = P for every automorphism φ of G — exactly the informal conclusion. The hypothesis `P : Sylow p G` with `hp : Nat.Prime p` captures "P a p-Sylow subgroup of G," and `hP : P.Normal` captures "P ⊲ G." The claim "a normal Sylow p-subgroup is characteristic" is true in arbitrary groups (no finiteness is needed at the statement level: one can show a normal Sylow p-subgroup is the unique Sylow p-subgroup even in infinite groups via the standard PQ-is-a-p-subgroup argument), so omitting a finiteness assumption doesn't make the Lean statement stronger. Hypotheses, quantifier structure, and mathematical objects all match.
